arts.ttl: Serie A | Monza v Napoli : Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Napoli will host Monza at the Stadio Diego Armando Maradona on Sunday, with both teams are coming off positive results in the Coppa Italia, with Napoli thrashing Palermo 5-0 and Monza eliminating Brescia with a 3-1 victory. The two sides find themselves in very different league positions early in the season. Napoli sit near the top of the table with 3 wins, 1 draw and 1 loss so far. Meanwhile, Monza are struggling in 19th place with just 3 points from 3 draws and 2 defeats. Napoli manager Antonio Conte has a mostly rested squad to choose from, having rotated The post Serie A | Monza v Napoli : Probable starting lineups appeared first on FootItalia.com.

Napoli have scored the most goals in the last 30 minutes of play in Serie A this season (7/9); on the other hand, only Como (4) have conceded more goals in the last 15 minutes than Monza (3 - level with Inter Milan, Parma and Torino). Among the teams currently in Serie A, Monza are on the longest winless run in league competition (14 games - D6 L8); the last non-promoted Lombard team to have recorded a longer streak of consecutive matches in the top flight without a win was Atalanta in 1994 (16).

arts.ttl: Exclusive: Chelsea fans given date they can expect “developments” on Victor Osimhen transfer
arts.sum: Chelsea will likely have to wait until closer to January before any developments on the Victor Osimhen transfer saga, according to Fabrizio Romano. arts.ttl: Chelsea told what Victor Osimhen ‘really wants’
arts.sum: Osimhen was subject to intense interest from several clubs, including Chelsea, Arsenal and PSG, but no deal could be struck with Napoli . The Premier League clubs with an interest in the Nigeria hitman, though, have been given an invitation to revisit the deal by Boniface.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Napoli v Monza: Match Preview
arts.sum: The Stadio Diego Armando Maradona is set to host a compelling Serie A clash on Sunday evening as high-flying Napoli welcome struggling Monza. Antonio Conte’s Napoli have made an impressive start to the 2024/25 campaign, sitting just one point off the top of the Serie A table after five matches. The Partenopei are unbeaten since the opening day and have been particularly ruthless in recent weeks, scoring nine unanswered goals in their last three outings across all competitions.
